source: Rocky Linux Admin Guide, ch. 14.5.3 dnf module (pp. 274-280)

questions.q: List all available streams of the nodejs module.
questions.why: dnf module list <name> shows the streams (and profiles) for that module.

questions.q: Enable stream 18 of the nodejs module (answering yes automatically).
questions.why: You must enable a stream before installing it; the syntax is dnf module enable <Module>:<Stream>.

questions.q: Install the nodejs module, stream 18, profile 'minimal' (auto-yes).
questions.why: dnf module install <Module>:<Stream>/<Profile> installs a specific stream and profile.

questions.q: Reset the nodejs module to its initial state (auto-yes).
questions.why: dnf module reset returns the module to its initial (neither enabled nor disabled) state.

questions.q: What does 'dnf module reset' do to already-installed packages?
options: Nothing - resetting the module does not change installed packages
It downgrades them to the default stream
questions.why: Reset only clears the module's enabled/disabled state; installed packages are left untouched.
dnf module disable <Module> turns the whole module off.

questions.q: After enabling one stream of a module, how many streams of that module can you use?
options: Only one - a module's default stream is active unless you disable it or enable a different stream
None until you install a profile
questions.why: Only a single stream of a module can be active; the default stream ([d]) is active unless you enable another or disable the module.
